mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent declines in the unemployment rate signal tight labor markets that boost corporate profits and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant positive correlation with UNRATE declines (Rank IC < 0.05) over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and housing starts indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant positive response to joint GDP-housing growth (t-test p > 0.10) or interaction effect size < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving supply chain efficiency and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 returns during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in industrial growth periods show negative or insignificant correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) with INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A 20%+ increase in real sales (RSAFS) relative to GDP growth signals strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant positive response to RSAFS/GDPC1 ratio spikes (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant negative correlation with CPIAUCSL spikes (Rank IC > -0.03) or t-test p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Discrepancies between payroll growth (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate changes signal labor market imbalances, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant negative response to labor market imbalances (t-test p > 0.10) or interaction effect < 0.12.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) during high consumer confidence regimes drive construction sector growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant positive correlation with HOUST in high RSAFS regimes (Rank IC < 0.04) or t-test p > 0.05.
